name: Ham with Port Raisin Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup double-strength beef broth, canned or homemade
2 tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Pierce the ham all over with the tip of a sharp knife.
2. In a 3 1/2 quart slow cooker, combine the port, broth and water.
3. Add the ham, cover and cook until a meat thermometer inserted into the center of the ham reads at least 165°F, 4 to 5 hours on low or 2 to 3 hours on high. Turn the ham once during cooking for even distribution of flavor.
4. Transfer the ham to a platter and cover with aluminum foil to keep warm.
5. Pour the cooking liquid into a medium saucepan, bring to a boil over high heat, and cook until the cooking liquid has reduced to 2 cups, about 5 minutes.
6. Stir in the raisins, brown sugar, and vinegar, reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Whisk in the cornstarch mixture and cook until just thickened. Pour the sauce into a sauceboat.
8. Slice the ham and serve with the sauce passed on the side.
Makes: 4 to 6 servings
